---
title: "Stroke Admission Orders"
---

Possible complications of IV tPA: intracranial hemorrhage and angioedema
Angioedema: Immediately assess for airway compromise, give 0.5 ml IM Epinephrine and 25 mg IV Benadryl
Intracranial hemorrhage: If the patientŌĆÖs clinical status deteriorates after administration of IV tPA, suspect
an intracranial bleed. CALL THE ATTENDING OR FELLOW IMMEDIATELY.
After administration of IV t-PA, monitor the patient for the following symptoms:
’éĘ Worsening level of consciousness (by GCS) or new/worsening neurological symptoms
’éĘ Nausea, vomiting, and/or sudden, severe headache
’éĘ Elevated blood pressure and/or bradycardia (the Cushing response, signs of increased ICP)
’éĘ Seizure
If intracranial hemorrhage is suspected:
’éĘ Obtain CT head stat
’éĘ Do not leave the patientŌĆÖs bedside until he/she is stable
If CT head shows an intracranial hemorrhage - DISCUSS IMMEDIATELY WITH STROKE ATTENDING:
’éĘ Obtain type & screen
’éĘ Tighten BP control, goal SBP 100-140, consider nicardipine infusion
’éĘ Check STAT fibrinogen and platelets
’éĘ If fibrinogen is <200 mg/dl, give 0.1 u/kg cryoprecipitate
’éĘ Consider urgent Neurosurgery consult
’éĘ If bleeding is refractory and/or life-threatening, consider aminocaproic acid 1 g/10 kg IV
Stroke Admission Orders and Workup: *exceptions to guidelines if tPA given
’éĘ For routine stroke admissions, use order set ŌĆ£NEU STROKE PROGRAM ISCHEMIC STROKE
ADMISSIONŌĆØ *Post-tPA or mechanical thrombectomy patients use order set ŌĆ£NEU STROKE
PROGRAM ORDERS ŌĆō POST ALTEPLASE (T-PA) ADMISSION ORDERS IP UFŌĆØ
’éĘ Bedside dysphagia screen performed by nursing, must be documented in chart. If the patient fails,
he/she MUST have formal swallow evaluation.
’éĘ Patient who fail dysphagia screen must be strict NPO (this includes oral medications!) until they
are cleared by speech/swallow
’éĘ Make sure any medication orders accurately reflect the patientŌĆÖs po status (eg oral or DHT route)
’éĘ ASA 325 mg PO if swallowing, 300 mg PR if not (*unless on anticoagulation or tPA in last 24 hours)
’éĘ Statin
’éĘ NS @ 100 cc/hr (careful with patients with CHF or on HD). Avoid hypotonic fluids.
’éĘ acetaminophen 650 mg q4h prn temp >100.4
’éĘ sliding scale insulin and fingersticks q6h
’éĘ neurochecks q4h on floor (q2h in IMC, q1h in NICU)
’éĘ UA/UDS
’éĘ CXR
’éĘ EKG in ED, telemetry/monitoring after admission
’éĘ SCD (all) plus Heparin SQ 5,000 units q8h (*unless tPA in last 24 hours)
’éĘ Permissive hypertension (SBP<220, DBP<110), hold home BP meds (except ┬Į dose ╬▓-blocker)
o Exception: BP goal will be lower following IV tPA (<180/105) or mechanical
thrombectomy (goal depends on procedure result, discuss with fellow/attending)
’éĘ Labs: lipid panel, HgbA1c, RPR, liver function tests, troponin, TSH, folate/B12
’éĘ If concerned for endocarditis, send serial blood cultures
’éĘ Transthoracic echocardiogram (+/- bubble study if <60 years old)
’éĘ MRI brain
’éĘ MRA head and neck vs. carotid ultrasound/transcranial dopplers if patient has not had CTA head/neck
**Certain patients (e.g., younger patients) may require TEE, hypercoagulable or vasculitis workup. Discuss
with attending before ordering.
Hypercoagulable panel: Antiphospholipid panel (anticardiolipin, DRVVT, beta-2 microglobulin, prothrombin gene
mutation, Factor V Leiden mutation, Protein C activity, Protein S activity, antithrombin III activity, HIT antibody
(in appropriate clinical setting).
